other_observers: Jared Del Rosso, Cole Sage (finder), Steve Stachowiak, M. Obs.
description: Finder was Cole SageBird was VERY active and would not stay still (difficult to photograph as it flitted, feeding primarily in leaf-less elm trees (I could not tell what the bird was feeding on...it must have be tiny).  The bird produced some call notes that Frank Farrell and I heard that were different from what we normally hear (and that led us to the bird on two occasions).Small warbler with sharply pointed bill.  Underparts were all clean whitish and all upper parts a pale gray.  The exception to this was a small dark red patch I saw on ONE occasion on the top of the head and on the rump and tail coverts (above the tail).  Black eyes and bill.  Otherwise, plumage was somewhat "unremarkable."eBird checklist with my photos showing the red rump is located at: https://ebird.org/checklist/S134108606
photographer: Photographer: Joey M. Kellner, Joey M. Kellner, Joey M. Kellner, Joey M. Kellner, Joey M. Kellner, Joey M. Kellner, Joey M. Kellner
